sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Very interesting! When the player moved LEFT:
1. Player moved from [28,33]-[31,35] to [28,29]-[31,31]
2. The c frame at [28,24]-[31,27] CHANGED COLOR from c to G!
3. The two b frame parts MERGED into one: [28,32]-[31,39] (18 pixels)
4. The two gray bar parts MERGED into one: [29,32]-[30,38] (14 pixels)
5. The b frame part at [28,28]-[31,31] and gray bar part at [29,29]-[30,31] DISAPPEARED (they were where the player moved to)
Key observations:
1. The c frame at [28,24]-[31,27] changed from c to G when the player moved adjacent to it (or through it). This is the same mechanic as before: when the player moves in a direction different from the facing direction, G frames convert to c frames. But here, a c frame converted to G!
2. The b frame and gray bar parts on the LEFT disappeared when the player moved into their area.
3. The b frame and gray bar parts on the RIGHT merged back together.
Wait, let me reconsider. The player moved from [28,33]-[31,35] to [28,29]-[31,31]. The b frame part at [28,28]-[31,31] was at the LEFT of the player's previous position. When the player moved LEFT, the player moved INTO the area of the b frame part at [28,28]-[31,31], causing it to disappear.
But the c frame at [28,24]-[31,27] is to the LEFT of the player's new position [28,29]-[31,31]. The c frame changed from c to G. This suggests that c frames convert to G when the player moves adjacent to them (or when the player's movement affects them).
Actually, I think the mechanic is:
1. When the player moves through a frame, the frame is cut/split.
2. When the player moves adjacent to a c frame (without moving through it), the c frame converts to G.
3. When the player moves away from a G frame, the G frame converts back to c.
But this doesn't quite match the earlier observations. Let me reconsider.
Looking at the sequence:
1. Initially, there was a c frame at [28,24]-[31,27].
2. After SPACE (transition 13), a G frame was created at [28,24]-[31,27].
3. After DOWN (transition 14), the G frame converted to c.
4. Now, after LEFT, the c frame converted back to G.
So the pattern is:
- SPACE creates a G frame at the target position.
- When the player moves in a different direction, G converts to c.
- When the player moves adjacent to a c frame (or through it?), c converts to G.
Wait, but in transition 14, the player moved DOWN and the G frame at [28,24]-[31,27] converted to c. The player was at [28,20]-[31,22] before moving DOWN, and the G frame was at [28,24]-[31,27]. So the player was to the LEFT of the G frame, and moved DOWN (away from the G frame). The G frame converted to c.
In the current transition, the player moved LEFT from [28,33]-[31,35] to [28,29]-[31,31]. The c frame was at [28,24]-[31,27]. The player moved closer to the c frame (from col 33-35 to col 29-31). The c frame converted to G.
So the pattern might be:
- When the player moves AWAY from a G frame, it converts to c.
- When the player moves TOWARD a c frame, it converts to G.
But this is getting complicated. Let me focus on the current state and figure out the goal.
Current state:
- c frame at [24,28]-[27,31] with blue box (unchanged)
- c frame at [28,16]-[31,19] with blue box (unchanged)
- G frame at [28,24]-[31,27] with blue box (was c, now G)
- b frame at [28,32]-[31,39] (merged, 18 pixels)
- gray bar at [29,32]-[30,38] (merged, 14 pixels)
- Player at [28,29]-[31,31]
The b frame and gray bar have been partially "eaten" by the player. The c frames are mostly unchanged, except one converted to G.
I think the goal might be to:
1. Eat all the frames (b frame, gray bar, c frames) by moving through them.
2. Or create a specific pattern of G/c frames.
Given the limited remaining actions (~22), I should focus on eating the remaining frames. The b frame and gray bar are at [28,32]-[31,39] and [29,32]-[30,38]. The player is at [28,29]-[31,31]. To eat the b frame and gray bar, I need to move RIGHT through them.
But wait, the b frame and gray bar are to the RIGHT of the player. Moving RIGHT would move the player into the b frame and gray bar, cutting/eating them.
Let me try moving RIGHT and see what happens.

action(['RIGHT'])
